obstruction_theorem: For any QA modulus m=p^k with p inert in Z[phi], the p-adic valuation v_p(r)=1 is a complete arithmetic obstruction: every such target r is unreachable, every obstruction-aware planner prunes it before search (nodes_expanded=0), and the pruning_ratio equals 1.0. This is certified by the chain [111] arithmetic â†’ [112] control bridge â†’ [113] planner correctness â†’ [114] efficiency savings â†’ [115] synthesis â†’ [116] validated report.

Cymatics (flatâ†’stripesâ†’hexagons) and seismology (quietâ†’p_waveâ†’surface_wave) both instantiate this orbit with k=2. synthesis_statement: QA is a kernel-governed system with two interacting public spines. The obstruction spine governs impossibility, pruning, and efficiency: arithmetic structure (v_p) propagates upward through control reachability, planner correctness, and efficiency savings to yield a zero-cost pruning guarantee. The control spine governs cross-domain compilation and orbit preservation: the same compiler law (singularityâ†’satelliteâ†’cosmos, k=2) governs structurally distinct physical domains. Both spines are rooted in QA_CORE_SPEC.v1 [107] and certified independently; together they constitute the first machine-verified dual-spine architecture in the QA ecosystem.
